content.problemStatement:
To make Yalalovichik even more satisfied and happy, Jafar decided to invent Yalalovichik strings. A string is called a Yalalovichik string if the set of all of its distinct non-empty substrings is equal to the set of all of its distinct non-empty subsequences.

You are given a string SSS. You need to find the number of its distinct non-empty substrings which are Yalalovichik strings.

**Note:** A string AAA is called a subsequence of a string BBB if AAA can be formed by erasing some characters (possibly none) from BBB. A string AAA is called a substring of a string BBB if it can be formed by erasing some characters (possibly none) from the beginning of BBB and some (possibly none) from the end of BBB. Two substrings or subsequences are considered different if they are different strings.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2Ōŗģ1062 \cdot 10^62Ōŗģ106
SSS contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer N=ŌłŻSŌłŻN = |S|N=ŌłŻSŌłŻ.
The second line contains the string SSS.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ŌĆö the number of distinct Yalalovichik substrings of SSS.

Explanation:
